pred_reason: The SWC-101 label is justified due to identified integer overflow issues in burnPercentage calculations, while SWC-107 applies because the contract contains multiple reentrancy vulnerabilities in external call patterns and uses dangerous low-level calls. Evidence: Rounding issues in burnPercentage calculations indicate integer overflow risks (Finding 8); Reentrancy vulnerabilities found in transfer functions with unsafe external calls (Finding 2); Use of call.value() patterns that enable reentrancy attacks (Finding 3)

raw.pred_reason: The SWC-101 label is justified due to identified integer overflow issues in burnPercentage calculations, while SWC-107 applies because the contract contains multiple reentrancy vulnerabilities in external call patterns and uses dangerous low-level calls. Evidence: Rounding issues in burnPercentage calculations indicate integer overflow risks (Finding 8); Reentrancy vulnerabilities found in transfer functions with unsafe external calls (Finding 2); Use of call.value() patterns that enable reentrancy attacks (Finding 3)
